get.summary: Get account information by Epic account ID.

parameters.description: One or more Epic account IDs (repeat the parameter for multiple).

parameters.description: External auth type (e.g. psn, xbl, nintendo, github).

parameters.name: displayName
parameters.description: Display name on the external platform.

parameters.name: caseInsensitive
parameters.description: Set to "true" to perform a case-insensitive lookup.

post.summary: Bulk lookup accounts by external display names.
Bulk lookup accounts by external platform IDs.

get.summary: Get all external auth connections for an account.
Get a specific external auth connection for an account.

Accepts one or more comma-separated Epic account IDs.

Get the paginated leaderboard for a specific event window.
Use leaderboardDef to resolve a cumulative or rank-tier leaderboard definition.

parameters.name: leaderboardDef
parameters.description: Optional leaderboard definition ID for cumulative or rank-tier views.

get.summary:
Get a player's event participation history.
Uses the V2 events endpoint (no /Fortnite/ prefix) with service token — no x-fortnite-token required.
If this endpoint returns 401/403 from Epic, the endpoint requires user auth after all.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Epic account ID of the player.

get.summary:
Find a player's rank and surrounding entries in an event window leaderboard.
Works for any placement including beyond top 10k. No x-fortnite-token required.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Epic account ID of the player to look up.

get.summary:
Get a player's standing in a specific event window.
Uses Epic's dedicated player endpoint — no leaderboard page scanning required.
Service token only, no x-fortnite-token needed.
GET /api/v2/events/{eventId}/windows/{eventWindowId}/players/{accountId}

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Epic account ID of the player.

get.summary: Get the Battle Royale inventory for a player.
Get the current keychain (used for Save the World trading).
Get purchase receipts for a player.
Get the list of enabled game features.
Get the current Fortnite version for a platform.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Optional version string to check against.

get.summary: Get privacy settings for a player. Requires x-fortnite-token.

get.summary: Get entitlements for the authenticated player. Requires x-fortnite-token.

parameters.name: accountId

parameters.name: friendId

parameters.description: Map version (patch number). Defaults to current version.

get.summary: Get map history entries, optionally filtered by chapter and/or season.

Initiate the device code OAuth flow.
Returns a flowId and a user-facing URL where the user must authenticate.
Poll POST /oauth/complete with the flowId to retrieve the token once the user has authorized.

post.summary:
Complete the device code OAuth flow by polling with the flowId returned from GET /oauth/get-token.
Returns 202 with AUTHORIZATION_PENDING while the user has not yet authenticated.
Returns 429 with RATE_LIMITED if polling too fast.
Refresh an Epic access token using a refresh token.
Exchange an authorization code for an Epic access token.
Re-authenticate silently using stored device auth credentials (accountId + deviceId + secret).

get.summary:
Returns the Epic Games authorization URL to redirect the user to.
The user will see Epic's standard login page — no scary device confirmation page.
After login, Epic redirects to your redirect_uri with ?code=...
Then call POST /oauth/link with that code to get the Fortnite access token + device auth.

post.summary:
Exchanges an Epic authorization code for a Fortnite access token and device auth credentials.
Use the code returned by Epic after the user authorizes via the URL from GET /oauth/authorize-url.
The returned deviceAuth (accountId + deviceId + secret) can be stored and used with
POST /oauth/refresh-device to silently re-authenticate in the future — no browser required.

Parse a single Fortnite .replay file and extract match statistics.
Subject to per-plan parsing quota limits.
post.description: Send the replay file as multipart/form-data with key "file".

post.summary:
Parse a single Fortnite .replay file and return only basic match statistics.
Significantly faster than full parsing — skips movement, zones, and kill feed.
Returns: name, replayId, version, stats (elims, damage, accuracy, placement, assists, damageTaken, damageStructures, matsFarm, matsUsed, totalPlayers).
post.description: Send the replay file as multipart/form-data with key "file".

post.summary:
Parse a single Fortnite .replay file and return full map context.
Returns: bus flight path + drop window, all storm circles with timing, supply drops, llamas, reboot vans.
post.description: Send the replay file as multipart/form-data with key "file".

post.summary:
Parse a single Fortnite .replay file and return ground loot data.
Returns all items that were on the ground near the player: position, item ID, picked-up status and time.

Parse a single Fortnite .replay file and return a time-ordered match timeline.
Returns all events relative to bus drop: kills, knocks, own death, damage dealt, damage taken, healed, pickups.
Requires full parse mode — slower than /parsing/stats but richer than /parsing.

Parse a single Fortnite .replay file and return storm zone data.
Returns all safe zone phases with circle positions, timing, damage per tick, and phase count.

Parse a single Fortnite .replay file and return the full player lobby.
Returns all players with placement, kills, death info, cosmetics, and team data.

Parse a single Fortnite .replay file and return the complete broadcast payload.
Combines all data: header, stats, full player lobby, storm zones, map objects, ground loot, and timeline events.
Requires full parse mode — equivalent to calling all endpoints in one request.

get.summary: Get all playlists (game modes).
Get currently active playlists.
Get a specific playlist by its ID.
Get raw Habanero track progress for a single account. Public — no token required.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Epic account ID of the player.

get.summary:
Get a player's XP, level, accountLevel, and battle pass tier.
Parsed from QueryProfile (profileId=athena) — requires x-fortnite-token.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Epic account ID of the player.

get.summary:
Get a player's enriched ranked progress — human-readable rank names, game mode labels,
season dates, and isCurrent flag.
Accepts either ?displayName= or ?accountId= (accountId skips the name lookup and is preferred).

parameters.name: displayName
parameters.description: Epic display name of the player (use accountId instead if available).

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Epic account ID of the player — preferred over displayName.

get.summary:
Get all available ranked game mode tracks — modes, division counts, and season dates.
Useful for building mode selectors or understanding what tracks exist.

parameters.name: endsBefore
parameters.description: Optional ISO 8601 date — only return tracks ending before this date.

parameters.name: endsAfter
parameters.description: Optional ISO 8601 date — only return tracks ending after this date.

post.summary:
Get ranked track progress for multiple account IDs in a single request (L3AGUE bulk endpoint).
Body: array of Epic account IDs.

Get a Habanero game leaderboard centered around an account (e.g. HazelnutSpread).
The gameId maps to the internal Habanero game identifier.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Epic account ID to center the leaderboard around.

parameters.name: findTeams
parameters.description: Whether to look up team scores (default: false).

get.summary: Get active quests and challenges for a player.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Epic account ID of the player.

get.summary:
Download a tournament .replay file by match ID.
Returns the raw .replay binary (application/octet-stream).
Match IDs come from Epic's tournament events API.

Get the raw chunk manifest (metadata) for a tournament replay.
Returns Events, DataChunks, Checkpoints arrays with timing info.

Download and fully parse a tournament replay by match ID.
Returns the same structure as POST /api/v1/parsing.
Subject to per-plan parsing quota limits (5 credits).

Download and parse a tournament replay — stats only.
Faster than full parse. Returns name, replayId, version, playlist, teamSize, teamCount, isTournament, tournamentRound, stats.
Subject to per-plan parsing quota limits (1 credit).

Download and parse a tournament replay — map context.
Returns bus path, storm circles, supply drops, llamas, reboot vans.
Subject to per-plan parsing quota limits (5 credits).

Download and parse a tournament replay — ground loot.
Server replays give whole-map loot coverage (vs ~150m radius for client replays).
Returns all item spawns: position, item ID, picked-up status and time.
Subject to per-plan parsing quota limits (5 credits).

Download and parse a tournament replay — full player lobby.
Returns all players with kills, placement, damage, reboots, headshots, teamKills, death info, and cosmetics.
Subject to per-plan parsing quota limits (5 credits).

Download and parse a tournament replay — storm zones.
Returns all safe zone phases with timing, positions, and damage per tick.
Subject to per-plan parsing quota limits (5 credits).

Download and parse a tournament replay — match timeline.
Returns chronological event feed: kills, knocks, death, damage dealt/taken, heals, pickups.
Subject to per-plan parsing quota limits (5 credits).

Download and parse a tournament replay — full broadcast payload.
Combines all data: stats, full lobby, storm zones, map objects, ground loot, and timeline.
Equivalent to calling all parse endpoints in one request.
Subject to per-plan parsing quota limits (20 credits).
Get the current Item Shop.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by item type (e.g. outfit, pickaxe).

parameters.name: rarity
parameters.description: Filter by rarity (e.g. rare, epic, legendary).

parameters.name: search

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Optional start time filter (Unix timestamp, e.g. 1773900001).

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: Optional end time filter (Unix timestamp, e.g. 1782950340).

parameters.name: stats
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of stat keys to include.

post.summary: Get stats for multiple players in one request.
post.description: Body should be an array of Epic account IDs or an object accepted by Epic's bulk stats endpoint.

get.summary: Get the global leaderboard for a specific stat.

parameters.name: stat

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return.

get.summary:
Get tournament tracker data for a player — token progression and past event participation.
Requires x-fortnite-token and accountId.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Epic account ID of the player.

get.summary:
Check a player's eligibility for a specific event — verifies all token requirements
(requireAllTokens, requireAnyTokens, requireNoneTokensCaller, etc.) when x-fortnite-token
is provided. Without the token, all requirements are listed as unverified.
Accepts display name or account ID (with or without dashes).

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Player display name or Epic account ID.

parameters.name: eventWindowId
parameters.description: Optional event window ID. Defaults to the most relevant window.

get.summary:
Check whether a player is eligible for tournaments based on their participation history.
Eligibility is determined by the number of tournaments played in the last N days.
Requires x-fortnite-token and accountId.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Epic account ID of the player.

parameters.name: requiredTournaments
parameters.description: Minimum number of tournaments required (1–50, default: 14).

get.summary:
Get the raw token set for one or more players directly from Epic's tokens endpoint.
Tokens represent eligibility flags earned by participating in tournaments.
Pass multiple IDs as comma-separated values: ?teamAccountIds=id1%2Cid2%2C...

Admin debug: returns the raw Epic /download response for a player.
Use this to diagnose why the tracker returns empty data.
Requires admin API key + x-fortnite-token.

Get the authenticated player's tournament data — token progression and event participation.
Requires x-fortnite-token and accountId.

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Optional region filter (e.g. EU, NAE, NAW).

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Epic account ID of the player.

get.summary: Get sessions for a specific event.
Get currently active and upcoming tournaments, enriched with CMS metadata.
Get all tournaments including past ones, enriched with CMS metadata.

Get all payout tables extracted from Epic's events download.
Player data is stripped — only reward/prize structures are returned.
Response is cached for 4 hours.
Get the payout table for a specific event window.

Get the top performers for a specific tracked stat across the entire leaderboard.
Fetches all pages in parallel batches so low-scoring but high-stat teams (e.g. top fraggers
in a placement-only event) are always included regardless of their leaderboard position.
Response includes an `availableStats` array listing every stat key tracked in this tournament.

parameters.name: statKey
parameters.description: Tracked stat key to rank by (e.g. TEAM_ELIMS_STAT_INDEX, DamageDealt, Headshots).

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Number of teams to return (default: 10, max: 100).

get.summary:
Get all tracked stats for a specific team identified by Epic account ID or display name.
Returns every stat tracked in this tournament for that team (total + per-game), plus their
rank in the requested statKey across all teams in the event.
Accepts account ID (32 hex chars) or display name — clan tags are stripped for matching.

parameters.name: statKey
parameters.description: Stat to show this team's rank in (e.g. DamageDealt, TEAM_ELIMS_STAT_INDEX).

parameters.name: teamIdentifier
parameters.description: Epic account ID or display name of any player on the team.

parameters.name: twitter
parameters.description: Admin only — return a plain-text Twitter snippet instead of JSON.

get.summary:
Get the Fortnite Power Rankings leaderboard — the official competitive skill rating.
            
Returns 100 players per page (100 pages total = top 10,000 players).
The service account used to query Epic is stripped from the response.
            
Supply x-fortnite-token + accountId to also receive the caller's own
PR entry as `playerEntry` in the response (null if they are unranked).
            
trackedStats keys: PR (score), countingEvents (max 20), peakPerf, deltaPR, peakPR.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Zero-indexed page (default: 0). 100 entries per page, 100 pages total.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Optional — Epic account ID of the caller. Required when x-fortnite-token is provided.

get.summary:
Search Power Rankings players by display name (partial, case-insensitive).
Results come from an in-memory index built from the full 10,000-player leaderboard.
The index is rebuilt every 2 hours and cached in Redis — no user token required.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Display name search term (partial match, min 2 chars recommended).

get.summary:
Look up a player's Power Rankings entry by display name or account ID.
Returns rank, pointsEarned, and trackedStats (PR, countingEvents, peakPerf, deltaPR, peakPR).
Accepts a 32-char hex account ID or an Epic display name (clan tags stripped automatically).

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Epic display name or account ID (e.g. KAMSLAM1x or b9b4aec...).

get.summary: Get a paginated tournament leaderboard for the given event window.
get.description:
The optional `leaderboardDef` parameter controls which leaderboard is returned:
            
**Cumulative leaderboard** — pass the def ID found in `eventWindow.scoreLocations`
where `isMainWindowLeaderboard = false` (e.g. `S40_FNCSMajor1_PlayInStage_CumulativeLeaderboardDef`).
The server forwards it as Epic's `sm=` query parameter, returning the combined
leaderboard across all sessions in the same group (Day 1 + Day 2, etc.).
            
**Ranked Cup (rank-tier)** — pass a def ID from `eventWindow.metadata.defaultLeaderboardByRank`
(e.g. `ranked_br_division_7`). The server resolves it to an internal window ID to filter
results by rank tier.
            
Include `x-fortnite-token` header for a personalized leaderboard view.

parameters.name: leaderboardDef
parameters.description: Optional leaderboard definition ID — cumulative def or rank-tier def.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Optional Epic account ID to highlight in the response.

get.summary: Get weapons for a given patch, with optional filters.

parameters.name: patch
parameters.description: Patch version (e.g. 32.00). Defaults to current patch.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search term to filter weapons by name.

parameters.name: rarity
parameters.description: Filter by rarity (e.g. rare, epic, legendary).

parameters.name: gamemode
parameters.description: Filter by game mode (e.g. BattleRoyale, ZeroBuild).

get.summary: Get all available weapon patches, with the current patch flagged.
Get rarity definitions and their display colors.

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Epic account lookup by ID, display name, display name resolution, and external auth providers.

tags.name: Aes
tags.description:
AES key and mappings endpoints.
GET /api/v1/aes and GET /api/v1/mappings are public.
POST /internal/aes/sync is Pi-only (X-Internal-Secret header).

tags.name: Assets
tags.description: Game asset bundle endpoints (tournaments, shop).

tags.name: CalendarSeason
tags.description: Current Fortnite season information.

tags.name: Cosmetics
tags.description: Fortnite cosmetics — browse, search, and retrieve individual items.

tags.name: Crew
tags.description: Fortnite Crew subscription — current pack and historical packs.

tags.name: EventsV2
tags.description: Event leaderboards. All data is fetched from Epic's leaderboard service using service auth.

tags.name: FortniteGame
tags.description:
Fortnite game data endpoints — inventory, keychain, receipts, version, privacy, and entitlements.
Endpoints that modify or read private player data require x-fortnite-token.

tags.name: Friends
tags.description: Friends list, friend requests, blocklist, and mutual friends.

tags.name: Map
tags.description: Fortnite map data, map images, and map history.

tags.name: News
tags.description: In-game news for Battle Royale, Save the World, and Creative modes.

tags.name: OAuth
tags.description:
Epic Games OAuth — device code flow, authorization code flow, token refresh, and account linking.
Rate limiting applies to most endpoints.

tags.name: Parsing (File Upload)
tags.description: Replay file parsing — extract match statistics from .replay files.

tags.name: Playlists
tags.description: Fortnite playlists (game modes).

tags.name: Profile
tags.description: Player profile: XP/level, ranked progress, game mode tracks, and Habanero leaderboards.

tags.name: Quests
tags.description: Player quest/challenge data. Requires x-fortnite-token.

tags.name: Replays (by Match ID)
tags.description: Tournament server replay download and parsing — retrieve and parse .replay files by match ID.

tags.name: Shop
tags.description: Item Shop and Battle Pass endpoints.

tags.name: Stats
tags.description: Player stats, bulk stats, and global leaderboards.

tags.name: TournamentTracker
tags.description: Tournament tracker and eligibility check endpoints. Requires x-fortnite-token.

tags.name: TournamentV1
tags.description:
Tournament listings, sessions, and leaderboards.
Player-specific endpoints require x-fortnite-token.

tags.name: Weapons
tags.description: Weapons, loot pool patches, and rarity definitions.
